title: haplopappus uniflorus var. howellii
intro: Haplopappus uniflorus var. howellii, also known as Howell's goldenrod, is a plant species within the aster family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, alleviating rheumatic pain, and providing relief from gastrointestinal issues due to its antispasmodic properties. Haplopappus uniflorus var. howellii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for internal use, or applied topically as a salve or ointment for skin issues.
